title: Coelorachis aurita
taxonomy: Coelorachis aurita is classified in the family Poaceae, which is a large and diverse family of grasses. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and morphological features. Further, it is placed in the subfamily Chloridoideae, which is a group of grasses that are commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The genus Coelorachis is also a part of this classification and is known to comprise a small number of species. The combination of these classifications provides a comprehensive understanding of Coelorachis aurita's place within the plant kingdom.

intro: Coelorachis aurita, also known as Coelorachis, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its reported health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Coelorachis aurita include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Coelorachis aurita is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in the form of capsules or tablets, to treat a range of health conditions.
